college.previous_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology (DA-IICT)
college.ugc_recognition: Included under Section 2(f) of the UGC Act; private university under Gujarat legislation

accreditation.status: Private university under Gujarat legislation; included under UGC Section 2(f)

accreditation.type: Government of Gujarat Centre of Excellence
accreditation.validity_period: Current recognition also evidenced in 2025 institutional material

accreditation.status: Participated; not present in published top/rank-band list used for this package

accreditation.note: Current institute-specific approval ID was not verified.
Current programme-wise NBA validity was not verified.

admission_routes.admission_process: Online application and institute counselling by JEE Main AIR and programme preference
admission_routes.eligibility: 10+2 with Mathematics, Physics and one of Chemistry/Biotechnology/Computer Science/Biology; minimum percentage as prescribed by AICTE
admission_routes.category_reservation: SC/ST candidates admitted on relaxed criteria; unfilled reserved seats may transfer to general

admission_routes.entrance_examination: ACPC/GUJCET merit under current ACPC rules
admission_routes.counselling_authority: Admission Committee for Professional Courses, Gujarat
admission_routes.admission_process: Separate ACPC application and state counselling
admission_routes.eligibility: As prescribed by ACPC for Gujarat-category degree engineering
admission_routes.spot_round: As announced by ACPC/University if vacancies remain

admission_routes.admission_process: Direct application under defined NRI/OCI/PIO/FN eligibility and official rounds
admission_routes.eligibility: NRI, OCI, PIO, foreign-national and eligible overseas-schooling definitions in the current admission page
admission_routes.special_round: Additional rounds if seats remain vacant

branch_change_rules.rule: ICT Honours students need CPI 6.5 after semester 3 to continue; eligible ICT students with CPI 7.5+ may apply for transfer subject to available seats and descending CPI.

fees.category_or_quota: All India and Gujarat Category – published base fee
fees.notes: Annual and four-year tuition are calculated at the published 2026 semester rate. Annual food is calculated as INR 5,500 x 12 months. Fees are subject to 8–10% annual revision, so no fixed four-year total is asserted. First-year current-rate institutional cash outflow excluding food is INR 490,600.
Includes INR 18,000 SHILP course after semester 2 and approximately INR 25,000 chip-design/fabrication course in semester 4. Additional value-added courses may be announced.

scholarships.academic_requirement: Top programme-wise students with SPI 8.5/10 or above
scholarships.benefit_description: Full semester tuition fee; five scholarships per batch
scholarships.renewal_conditions: Pass all credit courses; no backlog or disciplinary case; satisfy SPI each semester

scholarships.benefit_description: 70% semester tuition-fee reimbursement; eight scholarships per batch
scholarships.required_documents: Income certificate, tax/income documents and institute application
scholarships.renewal_conditions: Pass all courses; no backlog or disciplinary case; satisfy SPI and income rules

scholarships.benefit_description: 50% semester tuition-fee reimbursement; twenty scholarships per batch
scholarships.required_documents: Income certificate, tax/income documents and institute application
scholarships.renewal_conditions: Pass all courses; no backlog or disciplinary case; satisfy SPI and income rules

scholarships.entrance_exam_requirement: Top 3 JEE AIR holders within AIR 1–5000 and top 2 ACPC candidates within rank 1–25
scholarships.renewal_conditions: SPI 8.5 or above; pass courses; no backlog/disciplinary case; no other scholarship

scholarships.entrance_exam_requirement: Top five candidates with board rank 1–10
scholarships.renewal_conditions: SPI 8.5 or above; pass courses; no backlog/disciplinary case; no other scholarship

scholarships.entrance_exam_requirement: Top 4 female JEE candidates within AIR 1–10000 and top 1 female ACPC candidate within rank 1–50
scholarships.renewal_conditions: SPI 8.5 or above; pass courses; no backlog/disciplinary case; no other scholarship

scholarships.applicable_programme: ICT-domain B.Tech programmes; exact 2026 programme mapping requires confirmation
scholarships.domicile_requirement: Ahmedabad, Gandhinagar, Anand, Kheda or Vadodara district
scholarships.academic_requirement: Minimum 60% aggregate in Class X and XII; clear every semester
scholarships.benefit_description: 80% of tuition fee or INR 150,000 per year, whichever is lower; ten awards shared with eligible ICT/Law domain students
scholarships.required_documents: Application and financial/academic documents; home verification may occur
scholarships.renewal_conditions: Clear all courses; Trust decision and current terms

scholarships.applicable_programme: ICT, ICT Honours and MnC under the published cohort scheme
scholarships.applicable_year: Published for 2022 and 2023 batches; 2026 applicability unverified
scholarships.academic_requirement: SPI 7.0+ or CPI 6.5+ depending award category
scholarships.benefit_description: 25% semester tuition fee; four awards under published cohort rules
scholarships.required_documents: Income certificate, parent ITRs, declaration and undertaking
scholarships.renewal_conditions: No backlog or disciplinary case; no other scholarship; meet semester criterion

scholarships.applicable_programme: ICT, ICT Honours, MnC and EVD under published cohort scheme
scholarships.academic_requirement: SPI or CPI 7.0+ under the published year-wise award categories
scholarships.benefit_description: 25% semester tuition fee; four awards under published cohort rules
scholarships.required_documents: Income certificate, parent ITRs, declaration and undertaking
scholarships.renewal_conditions: No backlog or disciplinary case; no other scholarship; meet semester criterion

scholarships.applicable_programme: Eligible programmes and students under current scheme

scholarships.scholarship_name: National Scholarship Portal schemes potentially applicable
scholarships.benefit_description: Potentially applicable after matching the student to current official scheme rules

scholarships.scholarship_name: Digital Gujarat scholarship schemes potentially applicable

placements.notes: Placement percentage is calculated from NIRF students placed divided by students graduating in minimum stipulated time.
Placement percentage is calculated from NIRF students placed divided by students graduating in minimum stipulated time.
Placement percentage is calculated from NIRF students placed divided by students graduating in minimum stipulated time.
Salary values are Maximum Earning Potential, not fixed salary. The same report gives fixed-yearly-cash median 14.50 LPA, mean 12.09879 LPA and maximum 29.784 LPA.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.essential_technical_skills: DSA; object-oriented programming; systems fundamentals; web/backend or mobile development
company_skill_guidance.common_academic_subjects: Data structures, algorithms, operating systems, databases, networks
company_skill_guidance.tools_and_technologies: Git, Linux, one production language, testing, cloud basics
company_skill_guidance.suggested_projects: Build and deploy 2–3 substantial products; contribute to open source
company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: programming foundations. Year 2: DSA and one stack. Year 3: internships, systems depth and interview practice. Year 4: targeted revision and portfolio evidence.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.essential_technical_skills: Python; statistics; SQL; data cleaning; modelling; experimentation
company_skill_guidance.common_academic_subjects: Probability, statistics, linear algebra, machine learning
company_skill_guidance.tools_and_technologies: Python, pandas, scikit-learn, SQL, visualization tools
company_skill_guidance.communication_requirements: Explain assumptions, uncertainty and business impact
company_skill_guidance.suggested_projects: End-to-end analytics project with reproducible notebook and deployed model
company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: Python and math. Year 2: SQL/statistics. Year 3: ML projects and internships. Year 4: case studies and model evaluation.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.essential_technical_skills: Structured problem solving; market sizing; research; spreadsheet modelling
company_skill_guidance.suggested_projects: Industry-analysis deck and a measurable campus consulting project
company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: communication and campus exposure. Year 2: cases and Excel. Year 3: live projects/internships. Year 4: interview drills and sector depth.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.essential_technical_skills: User research; metrics; prioritization; experimentation; technical literacy
company_skill_guidance.tools_and_technologies: Analytics, prototyping, roadmapping and collaboration tools
company_skill_guidance.suggested_projects: Ship a product with user interviews, metrics and iteration evidence
company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: build and observe users. Year 2: analytics/design basics. Year 3: product internships. Year 4: product cases and portfolio.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.essential_technical_skills: Digital/analog design; HDL; microcontrollers; verification; signal basics
company_skill_guidance.common_academic_subjects: Digital electronics, analog circuits, computer architecture, signals
company_skill_guidance.tools_and_technologies: Verilog/SystemVerilog, FPGA tools, SPICE, C/C++, lab instruments
company_skill_guidance.suggested_projects: FPGA/ASIC block, embedded device or verification project
company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: circuits/programming. Year 2: digital/analog labs. Year 3: HDL/embedded specialization and internship. Year 4: verification/design interviews.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.essential_technical_skills: Power systems; machines; control; protection; simulation
company_skill_guidance.common_academic_subjects: Electrical machines, power electronics, control systems
company_skill_guidance.tools_and_technologies: MATLAB/Simulink, ETAP/PSCAD where available, CAD and instrumentation
company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: math/circuits. Year 2: machines/control. Year 3: simulation and industrial internship. Year 4: domain interviews and standards.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.essential_technical_skills: CAD; mechanics; thermal/fluid fundamentals; manufacturing; quality
company_skill_guidance.common_academic_subjects: Thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, design, manufacturing processes
company_skill_guidance.tools_and_technologies: CAD/CAE, MATLAB/Python, GD&T, basic CNC/process tools
company_skill_guidance.suggested_projects: Designed-and-built mechanism, thermal study or process-improvement project
company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: workshop and CAD. Year 2: core mechanics. Year 3: CAE/manufacturing internship. Year 4: design portfolio and domain preparation.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.common_academic_subjects: Structures, soil mechanics, transportation, construction management
company_skill_guidance.tools_and_technologies: CAD/BIM, structural or geotechnical software, spreadsheets
company_skill_guidance.communication_requirements: Site communication, reporting and safety awareness
company_skill_guidance.suggested_projects: Structural model, transport analysis or sustainable-infrastructure study
company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: drafting and mechanics. Year 2: core civil subjects. Year 3: software/site internship. Year 4: portfolio, codes and project cases.

company_skill_guidance.label: General career guidance; not a DAU- or recruiter-specific verified requirement
company_skill_guidance.essential_technical_skills: Mass/energy balances; transport; process control; safety; quality
company_skill_guidance.common_academic_subjects: Thermodynamics, reaction engineering, transport phenomena, process design
company_skill_guidance.tools_and_technologies: Process simulators, MATLAB/Python, laboratory and quality tools

company_skill_guidance.preparation_roadmap: Year 1: chemistry/math. Year 2: balances/thermo. Year 3: simulation/lab internship. Year 4: process design and industry-specific preparation.

hostels_and_facilities.hostel_allotment_policy: On-campus hostel is for B.Tech programmes; B.Tech residence is described as compulsory in the current FAQ.
hostels_and_facilities.hostel_availability_guarantee: Published as the B.Tech residential arrangement; exceptions require confirmation
hostels_and_facilities.mess_and_food: Seven outsourced food courts; food charged on actuals, approximately INR 5,500 per month in 2026 admissions
hostels_and_facilities.library: Two Wi-Fi buildings, about 16,000 sq ft, 450-user capacity and extensive print/e-resources
hostels_and_facilities.medical_centre: Campus Medical Centre with scheduled doctor visits and free consultation/dispensed medicines
hostels_and_facilities.ambulance_or_emergency_transport: Institute vehicle and driver on 24x7 emergency duty
hostels_and_facilities.hostel_rules: Ragging, alcohol and smoking prohibited; detailed current rulebook requires review
hostels_and_facilities.medical_insurance: Official page states group mediclaim coverage of INR 2.50 lakh per annum subject to procedures

location.locality: Near Reliance Cross Road / Infocity area
location.nearest_airport: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad
location.technology_ecosystem: Infocity Gandhinagar is nearby; GIFT City and Ahmedabad technology/startup ecosystems are accessible by road.
location.nearby_student_housing: PG and rental options exist in Gandhinagar/Infocity, but B.Tech residence is officially compulsory.
location.cost_of_living_band: Moderate for Gandhinagar; campus food and travel choices affect spend
location.data_origin: Official broad travel statements plus map-derived estimates

senior_junior_culture.summary: Recent student sources describe hostel friendships, senior advice and extensive student-led clubs and committees. Evidence suggests regular cross-batch interaction, but formal mentorship quality and club hierarchy are not sufficiently documented for a universal conclusion.
positive_themes: freshers receive practical hostel and academic advice
negative_themes: language-group experiences differ for some out-of-state students

academic_culture.summary: A strong repeated student signal describes demanding coursework, frequent assessment and comparatively difficult grading. The official research-oriented curriculum and technical environment are positives, while students disagree on how comfortably academics can be balanced with clubs and placements.
negative_themes: limited free time for some students

hostel_reality.summary: Official evidence confirms compulsory B.Tech residence, furnished non-AC rooms, free internet, hot water, laundry and three halls with calculated capacity around 1,901. Student evidence is broadly positive about hygiene and community but mixed on cost, women's washroom capacity and room/block variation.
positive_themes: hostel friendships and late-night peer life
negative_themes: individual room and block condition varies

club_and_campus_life.summary: The official student-affairs page lists an extensive student-run ecosystem spanning technical, cultural, sports, media and governance activities. Several students describe active clubs, fests and concerts, while a smaller set reports limited campus life, creating a conflicting but overall moderately positive signal.
negative_themes: individual experiences of activity level differ
some students report low language diversity

coding_and_placement_culture.summary: Coding and competitive-programming culture is a strong repeated positive signal supported by official technical clubs and student accounts. Placement evidence is comparatively transparent for 2023-24 through an audited report, but students disagree about recent cycles and the meaning of headline averages.
negative_themes: headline salary definitions can be misunderstood
student concern about current placement transparency
coding_and_placement_culture.evidence_strength: Strong repeated signal with placement conflict

locality_and_student_life.summary: The campus is in Gandhinagar near Infocity, with GIFT City and Ahmedabad accessible by road. Students generally value the green, quiet campus and food options; city exposure is less immediate than a central urban campus, while nearby hospitals and technology areas improve practicality.
negative_themes: deliveries may stop at the main gate
out-of-state language comfort varies by peer group

overall_satisfaction.summary: Public student evidence is mixed but often positive about coding culture, campus environment, peer learning, food and residential life. The most repeated concerns are academic pressure, fee growth, non-AC residence, language diversity for some students and uncertainty about recent placement cycles. No numeric culture score is justified.

parent_mode_summary.total_cost: Current base tuition is INR 192,500 per semester, plus registration, hostel, food and deposit. Annual revision of 8–10% prevents a reliable fixed four-year total.
parent_mode_summary.scholarship_possibilities: Strong institutional merit/fellowship and merit-cum-means options exist, but awards are limited, competitive and renewal-based.
parent_mode_summary.placement_stability: NIRF and audited records use different denominators. The audited 2023-24 eligible pool reports all 264 accepted offers, while NIRF reports 265 placed among 358 graduating.
parent_mode_summary.travel_distance: Gandhinagar location is near Infocity and accessible from Ahmedabad airport; road traffic affects travel time.
parent_mode_summary.hostel_availability: B.Tech students are expected to live on campus; official capacity totals about 1,901 across three halls.
parent_mode_summary.medical_access: Campus medical centre, two nearby hospital arrangements and 24x7 emergency vehicle are officially published.
parent_mode_summary.academic_discipline: Student evidence strongly indicates demanding coursework and grading.
parent_mode_summary.source_confidence: Admissions, ACPC cutoffs, fees, placement reports and hostel capacities are strongly sourced; All India cutoffs, NBA status and branch-wise placement outcomes are missing.
major_unresolved_risks: no official All India historical cutoff archive

student_mode_summary.branch_fit: ICT and ICT-CS combine software, communication and electronics; MnC is mathematics-heavy; EVD is semiconductor/VLSI-focused; CS-AI and ECE-AI are new and lack graduate outcomes.
student_mode_summary.coding_core_culture: Official Programming, AI, cybersecurity, IEEE and research organisations align with repeated reports of strong coding culture.
student_mode_summary.clubs_and_campus_life: Student-run technical, cultural, sports and governance activity is broad, though individual reports conflict about vibrancy and language diversity.
student_mode_summary.academic_flexibility: Rigorous programmes and transfer rules reward strong CPI; students repeatedly report high workload.
student_mode_summary.hostel_experience: Residential peer life is a major positive; rooms are non-AC and block-level experiences vary.
student_mode_summary.internships: Official curricula and placement ecosystem provide industry exposure, but programme/branch internship counts are not public.
student_mode_summary.city_exposure: Gandhinagar is quieter than central Ahmedabad; Infocity and GIFT City are nearby opportunities.
student_mode_summary.placement_roadmap: Protect CPI, build DSA/development or electronics/VLSI depth early, participate in technical clubs, and compare fixed cash with maximum earning potential.
major_risks: joining new branches without historical outcomes
assuming audited eligible-pool rate equals whole-cohort rate

decision_risks.risk: No official All India JEE historical closing ranks
New CS-AI and ECE-AI programmes have no graduate placement history
Scholarship awards are limited and conditional
Software preference with EVD/ECE-AI without confirming role eligibility

data_gaps: Official All India JEE Main closing ranks for 2023–2025 were not found.
Official 2026 admission cutoffs are not final.
Current AICTE institute ID/applicability was not verified.
Current programme-wise NBA accreditation validity was not verified.
Branch-wise placement percentages and salary outcomes are unavailable.
Recruiter-specific roles, branch eligibility and selected counts are unavailable.
CS-AI and ECE-AI have no graduating-batch placement history.
NRI/FN fee table was not normalised in this package.
Current hostel curfew, outing rules and residential exceptions require confirmation.
Current accessibility, campus transport, ATM and shop details are incomplete.
Several government scholarship benefits and deadlines require profile-specific verification.

conflicts.source_1_value: Current official name: Dhirubhai Ambani University (DAU)
conflicts.source_2_value: Historical/NIRF records use Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology (DA-IICT)
conflicts.possible_reason: Government of Gujarat renamed the institution in 2024; external systems retain the historical name
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Use Dhirubhai Ambani University as current name and preserve DA-IICT as previous/common name

conflicts.source_1_value: FAQ text refers to four undergraduate programmes while listing six programmes
conflicts.source_2_value: 2026 admissions page publishes six B.Tech programmes
conflicts.possible_reason: Stale introductory wording after two programmes were added for 2026-27
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Use the six-programme 2026 admissions list; flag the FAQ wording for correction
conflicts.status: resolved by detailed current programme list

conflicts.source_1_value: NIRF reports sanctioned UG intake of 432 for 2021-22 to 2023-24
conflicts.source_2_value: Calculated 2026 total is 520 seats across All India, Gujarat and DAFS routes
conflicts.possible_reason: Programme expansion and launch of CS-AI and ECE-AI in 2026
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Store year-specific intake; never compare the values as if they describe the same admission cycle

conflicts.source_1_value: NIRF: 358 graduating, 265 placed, median salary INR 18.00 lakh
conflicts.source_2_value: Audited eligible pool: 264 eligible and 264 accepted offers; median maximum earning potential INR 16.0086 lakh
conflicts.possible_reason: Different denominators, reporting populations and salary definitions
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Preserve both records with scope and salary-head labels; do not merge into one placement percentage

conflicts.source_1_value: Historical official material reflects an earlier lower semester fee
conflicts.source_2_value: 2026 published tuition is INR 192,500 per semester and subject to 8–10% annual revision
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Use only year-labelled fee records and avoid a fixed four-year all-inclusive total

conflicts.source_1_value: Current institute scheme applies from the 2025-26 batch with 33 awards
conflicts.source_2_value: Jai Jhulelal page is explicitly framed for 2022 and 2023 batches
conflicts.possible_reason: Different donors, cohorts and scholarship regulations
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Treat alumni schemes as cohort-specific until a 2026 notice confirms eligibility

conflicts.source_1_value: One current-student account reports limited campus life and out-of-state discomfort
conflicts.source_2_value: Multiple replies and alumni describe active clubs, festivals and positive residential life
conflicts.possible_reason: Personal social circles, cohort, language comfort and participation level
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Publish as a conflicting student-reported signal, not a college-wide fact

conflicts.source_1_value: Public discussion disputes the representativeness of placement claims
conflicts.source_2_value: Other community replies report strong opportunities and improving cycles
conflicts.possible_reason: Different batches, recession effects, second-hand information and salary-definition confusion
conflicts.recommended_manual_action: Use audited/NIRF records for facts and keep current-cycle commentary qualitative

manual_verification_required: Confirm legal name, previous name and database aliases.
Confirm final 2026 seat matrix and any supernumerary/TFWS seats.
Add final 2026 ACPC cutoffs after counselling closes.
Ask DAU whether historical All India JEE closure ranks can be released.
Verify current AICTE and programme-wise NBA records.
Reconcile NIRF and audited placement scope before UI display.
Confirm current NRI/FN fees and scholarship rules.
Confirm current hostel rulebook, curfew and exceptions.
Recheck map-derived travel distances and metro interchange.
